svg.text: RBMK-1000 REACTOR — CHERNOBYL UNIT 4 · ENGINEERING FAILURE ANALYSIS
26 APRIL 1986 · 01:23:40 LOCAL TIME · PRIPYAT, UKRAINIAN SSR · UNCONTROLLED PROMPT-SUPERCRITICAL EXCURSION
Rate of rise: ~400–1000 MW/s by t+6 s · Rate ≈ (ρ−β)/Λ · P
Rod speed: 0.4 m/s → full insertion in 18 seconds
Post-accident retrofit: 12 s + 24 fast rods >2β in <2.5 s
Design: +void coeff · positive scram · slow rods · weak containment
Operations: low ORM · 200 MW(th) test · ECCS isolated
→ Shutdown system became the initiator of runaway
Why did the system permit it?
Design defects undisclosed · No independent regulator
→ Deficient safety culture across design, ops, and govt
Sources: INSAG-7 (IAEA, 1992) · World Nuclear Association · Convention on Early Notification of a Nuclear Accident (IAEA, 1986) · Convention on Nuclear Safety (IAEA, 1994)
